summary: Matched the common fighter DamageSong translation unit by replacing the asm object with the C object and marking ftCo_DamageSong.c as Matching. The substantive C-side matching adjustment was small: remove explicit local literal constant declarations and change helper inlineA0 from `static inline float` to `static float`, after which the 340-line asm file for ftCo_DamageSong was deleted.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageSong.s`, removing 340 lines of hand-maintained assembly for the DamageSong common fighter action code.
Changed `configure.py` so `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageSong.c` is built as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` so the build links `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageSong.c.o` instead of `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageSong.s.o`.
In `ftCo_DamageSong.c`, removed explicit literal definitions for `ftCo_804D8CC0`, `ftCo_804D8CC8`, and `ftCo_804D8CCC`.
In `ftCo_DamageSong.c`, changed helper `inlineA0(ftCo_Fighter* fp, bool arg1)` from `static inline float` to `static float`.
smart_moves: The final source delta was intentionally minimal: one helper storage/inline qualifier change plus removal of manual literal symbols, reducing risk while enabling the file to match.
The PR completed the standard decomp workflow by updating both matching metadata (`configure.py`) and the legacy object list (`obj_files.mk`) at the same time as deleting the asm fallback.
The change suggests the author isolated a codegen-sensitive mismatch to inline behavior around `inlineA0` rather than restructuring the larger DamageSong logic.
decomp_lessons: For small remaining mismatches, test whether an explicitly `static inline` helper should instead be a plain `static` helper; inline qualifiers can materially affect generated PowerPC code.
Do not keep hand-authored literal symbols if the compiler can emit the needed constants correctly from the C expression; redundant literal declarations may block matching.
When a file matches, remove the corresponding asm file and switch all build references from `asm/...s.o` to `src/...c.o` in the same PR.
Even when the matched asm contains many action callbacks, the actual matching fix may be a tiny codegen-control adjustment rather than a semantic rewrite.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_DamageSong.c` follows common fighter action naming with `ftCo_` prefix.
Action callback names use the pattern `ftCo_DamageSong_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageSong_IASA`, `ftCo_DamageSong_Phys`, and `ftCo_DamageSong_Coll`.
Related substates are named with suffixes such as `DamageSongWait` and `DamageSongRv`, each with the same `Anim`/`IASA`/`Phys`/`Coll` callback pattern.
Unidentified or address-based functions are retained with names such as `ftCo_800C318C`, `ftCo_800C3390`, `ftCo_800C3480`, `ftCo_800C3538`, and `ftCo_800C3598`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an asm fallback after the C translation unit matched.
Changed `inlineA0` from `static inline` to plain `static`, indicating inline control was the decisive matching lever visible in the diff.
Removed explicit `S32_TO_F32`, `0.0f`, and `1.0f` literal declarations, likely allowing compiler-generated literal placement to match naturally.

The deleted asm showed typical DamageSong behavior involving `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining`, `ftCommon_8007DBCC`, `ftCommon_8007DC08`, `ft_80084F3C`, and `ft_80084280`; the PR does not provide detailed commentary beyond the diff.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCommon matching PRs where changing `static inline` to `static` fixed codegen.
Search for remaining explicit local literal definitions that may be unnecessary once a file is close to matching.
Compare DamageSong callback naming against other common action files with Wait/Rv substates.
Audit build-list updates in matching PRs to ensure both `configure.py` and `obj_files.mk` are changed when asm is removed.
